record.field: n8n + Worker + Runner with Redis/Postgres and Ollama
n8n is a fair-code workflow automation platform with native AI, here deployed in production-grade queue mode with local LLMs via Ollama.

n8n is a fair-code workflow automation platform with native AI, here deployed in production-grade queue mode with local LLMs via Ollama.

n8n connects any app, API or database through a visual node editor combined with real JavaScript or Python code. This deployment runs the scalable queue-mode architecture: a main process serves the UI and triggers, dedicated worker processes execute workflows, and sandboxed task runners isolate Code-node execution. Redis acts as the job queue, PostgreSQL holds workflow definitions, encrypted credentials and execution history, and a bundled Ollama service provides self-hosted LLMs for AI agents and RAG without sending data to third-party clouds.

Key features
  • Visual drag-and-drop workflow builder with 400+ integrations and native AI/LangChain agent nodes
  • Scalable queue mode: separate main, worker and webhook processes for distributed, reliable execution
  • Sandboxed task runners that isolate JavaScript/Python Code nodes from the n8n process and host
  • Redis-backed job queue (Bull) with retries and dead-letter handling for resilient runs
  • PostgreSQL as shared persistent state for workflows, encrypted credentials and execution history
  • Self-hosted local LLMs via Ollama for private AI workflows, agents and retrieval-augmented generation
Best for

Teams and developers who need a self-hosted, privacy-preserving automation hub that scales to high execution volumes and runs AI workflows on local models, as an open alternative to Zapier or Make.

services: n8n: image: n8nio/n8n:latest restart: unless-stopped environment: - NODE_ENV=production - N8N_HOST={{ n8n_host }} - N8N_PORT={{ n8n_port }} - N8N_PROTOCOL=https - DB_TYPE=postgresdb - DB_POSTGRESDB_HOST=postgres - DB_POSTGRESDB_USER={{ postgres_user }} - DB_POSTGRESDB_PASSWORD={{ postgres_password }} - N8N_DIAGNOSTICS_ENABLED=false - N8N_PERSONALIZATION_ENABLED=true - N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY={{ encryption_key }} - N8N_USER_MANAGEMENT_JWT_SECRET - N8N_SECURE_COOKIE=true - N8N_ENFORCE_SETTINGS_FILE_PERMISSIONS=true - N8N_BLOCK_ENV_ACCESS_IN_NODE=true - N8N_PROXY_HOPS=${PROXY_HOPS:-1} - WEBHOOK_URL=https://{{ n8n_host }}/ - GENERIC_TIMEZONE={{ generic_timezone }} - N8N_TEMPLATES_ENABLED=true - N8N_HIRING_BANNER_ENABLED=false - OFFLOAD_MANUAL_EXECUTIONS_TO_WORKERS=true - OLLAMA_HOST=${OLLAMA_HOST:-ollama:11434} - N8N_RUNNERS_ENABLED=true - N8N_RUNNERS_MODE=external - N8N_RUNNERS_BROKER_LISTEN_ADDRESS=0.0.0.0 - N8N_RUNNERS_AUTH_TOKEN={{ n8n_runners_secret }} - N8N_NATIVE_PYTHON_RUNNER=true - N8N_RUNNERS_MAX_CONCURRENCY=50 - EXECUTIONS_DATA_PRUNE=true - EXECUTIONS_DATA_MAX_AGE=7 - EXECUTIONS_MODE=queue - QUEUE_BULL_REDIS_HOST=redis - QUEUE_BULL_REDIS_PORT=6379 volumes: - ./n8n_data:/home/node/.n8n depends_on: postgres: condition: service_healthy redis: condition: service_healthy container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:5678' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network n8n-worker: image: docker.n8n.io/n8nio/n8n:latest restart: always command: worker environment: - NODE_ENV=production - N8N_HOST={{ n8n_host }} - N8N_PORT={{ n8n_port }} - N8N_PROTOCOL=https - EXECUTIONS_MODE=queue - DB_TYPE=postgresdb - DB_POSTGRESDB_HOST=postgres - DB_POSTGRESDB_USER={{ postgres_user }} - DB_POSTGRESDB_PASSWORD={{ postgres_password }} - N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY={{ encryption_key }} - N8N_SECURE_COOKIE=true - N8N_ENFORCE_SETTINGS_FILE_PERMISSIONS=true - N8N_BLOCK_ENV_ACCESS_IN_NODE=true - N8N_PROXY_HOPS={{ proxy_hops }} - WEBHOOK_URL=https://{{ n8n_host }}/ - GENERIC_TIMEZONE={{ generic_timezone }} - QUEUE_BULL_REDIS_HOST=redis - QUEUE_BULL_REDIS_PORT=6379 depends_on: - n8n - redis container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_n8n_worker' networks: - internal n8n-runner: image: n8nio/runners:nightly restart: always environment: - N8N_RUNNERS_TASK_BROKER_URI=http://n8n:5679 - N8N_RUNNERS_AUTH_TOKEN={{ n8n_runners_secret }} - N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY={{ encryption_key }} - GENERIC_TIMEZONE={{ generic_timezone }} - N8N_RUNNERS_MAX_CONCURRENCY=50 depends_on: - n8n container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_n8n_runner' networks: - internal ollama: image: ollama/ollama:latest restart: unless-stopped volumes: - ./ollama_storage:/home/node/.ollama container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_ollama' networks: - internal init-ollama: image: ollama/ollama:latest volumes: - ./ollama_storage:/home/node/.ollama entrypoint: /bin/sh environment: - OLLAMA_HOST=ollama:11434 command: - -c - sleep 3; ollama pull llama3.2:latest; ollama pull gemma3:latest container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_init_ollama' restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal redis: image: redis:7-alpine restart: unless-stopped command: redis-server --appendonly yes healthcheck: test: - CMD - redis-cli - ping interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 volumes: - ./redis_storage:/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_redis' networks: - internal postgres: image: postgres:17-alpine hostname: postgres restart: unless-stopped environment: - POSTGRES_USER={{ postgres_user }} - POSTGRES_PASSWORD={{ postgres_password }} - POSTGRES_DB={{ postgres_db }} volumes: - ./postgres_storage:/var/lib/postgresql/data healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready -h localhost -U {{ postgres_user }} -d {{ postgres_db }} interval: 5s timeout: 5s retries: 10 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_postgres' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d postgres for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
